notes.anchor: the matter is tending toward arms
notes.note: Written in October 50 BC as Cicero returned from governing Cilicia, this line registers the slide toward civil war between Caesar and Pompey, which broke out when Caesar crossed the Rubicon in January 49 BC.
T. Pomponius Atticus, Cicero's lifelong friend and chief correspondent, here asked to manage a property auction in Cicero's absence. Atticus was Rome's most reliable financial fixer for his friends.
The Ides fell on the 15th in March, May, July, and October, and on the 13th in every other month; so "the day before the Ides of October" is 14 October 50 BC, when Cicero reached Athens.
